reason: Invariants are not well-chosen: they duplicate a core relation (m9 == p+4 and m9 == at(p,Pre)+4) instead of a single core invariant; an invariant like 'uh9 >= at(p,Pre)+4' is not inductive (not preserved by the loop body under the guard) and can be violated by reachable transitions; consequently the set is not inductive or clean; should replace with a single core invariant like m9 == p+4 and a tight relation q == d - 4 and m9 - uh9 >= 0 (i.e., uh9 <= m9) with proper loop-assigns.

Non inductive due to incorrect f relation: (zc30 > 0) ==> (f == y - zc30 + 1) is false after first iteration; actual update is f == y - zc30. Correct invariant would be (zc30 > 0) ==> (f == y - zc30). Other invariants are valid (l == \at(l, Pre); u0 == y; 0 <= zc30 <= y; zc30 == 0 ==> f == 4).

No explicit loop variant/termination bound; invariants do cover modified vars but lack a proven decreasing measure (e.g., qle - v0g) to prove termination; some invariants are tautological given g == 0 (e.g., b == \at(b, Pre)) and could be tightened; overall not high quality.

The invariant ((y4 >= 1) ==> (q238 + y4 == fa1 + 1)) is false; in fact q238 + y4 == fa1 for all y4, since q238 = fa1 - y4. Thus the set is not inductive. The other invariants (fa1 - c == 23 and fa1 == at(c,Pre) + 23) are redundant but correct; the main issue is the incorrect one.

Missing loop variant/decreasing measure to prove termination. Invariants correctly relate qwf and kngh to l7cb and bound l7cb by vm, but there is no loop_variant expression (e.g., vm - l7cb) that decreases each iteration. Therefore termination argument is incomplete. The invariants about e and u are harmless but unnecessary; no other major issues.
